text.title: Onboarding Hard Rules and Antipatterns
text.summary: Concrete, testable rules for email sequencing, microcopy, and step gating, plus antipatterns that silently kill activation.

rule.statement: Kill the triggered email sequence the moment the user fires the activation event. No exceptions. Every campaign must subscribe to the activation event and exit immediately on fire.
rule.rationale: Email sequences that fire after activation produce embarrassing "still need help?" messages to already-engaged users, which damages trust and increases unsubscribes.

rule.statement: Structure the email sequence as: Email 1 (immediate) = welcome + one action; Email 2 (Day 1) = quick-win tutorial; Email 3 (Day 3) = feature highlight; Email 4 (Day 7) = success story. Stop sending when the user activates.
rule.rationale: Mid-trial check-in on day 7 of a 14-day trial outperforms higher-frequency early emails; users need quiet space to explore before being nudged.

rule.statement: Cap all tooltip and in-app guidance copy at 80 characters. Use neutral/direct voice — no exclamations, no emojis, no "Welcome aboard!" filler.
rule.rationale: Appcues and Pendo enforce character limits. Exclamatory copy trends toward corporate-speak that users dismiss without reading.

rule.statement: Limit tooltip tours to a maximum of 5 items in a single session. More than 8 tooltips trigger dismiss-and-forget behavior.
rule.rationale: Users stop reading tooltip sequences above ~5; every additional tooltip reduces completion of all subsequent tooltips.

rule.statement: Never gate the activation event behind profile completion, payment info, or optional preferences. Make those steps available but not required before the user reaches first value.
rule.rationale: Required profile completion blocks the activation event and tanks Day-0 retention — the single strongest predictor of eventual conversion.

rule.statement: Run a no-onboarding control cell (5% of new signups) to measure the true incremental lift of the onboarding flow. Check this quarterly.
rule.rationale: Onboarding sometimes adds zero net activation lift; the answer can only be found by measuring against no-onboarding. Teams that skip this sometimes discover they are adding friction, not removing it.

antipattern.description: Tooltip tour with 8+ stops shown in the first session.
antipattern.reason: Users dismiss the entire sequence after the third tooltip; critical guidance for later steps is never seen.

antipattern.description: A/B testing onboarding without segmenting by user type.
antipattern.reason: Segment X gains and segment Y losses cancel out; the test appears flat even when one group benefits significantly.

antipattern.description: Empty state with multiple "create / import / use template" calls to action of equal weight.
antipattern.reason: Users who face a choice with no hierarchy think instead of act. One primary action + two secondary fallbacks is the correct pattern.

text.reference: Bash validator — fails if any step lacks a defined analytics event
